key_insight: Detonation-sprayed Al2O3-based coatings on stainless steel achieved a strong balance of hardness, corrosion resistance, and wear performance, with coating properties strongly influenced by barrel filling level.
engineering_relevance: Optimizing detonation-spray parameters, especially barrel filling around 58–63%, can improve protective ceramic coatings for wear- and corrosion-prone components.
method: Al2O3-based oxide coatings were deposited on 10Kh18N9 austenitic stainless steel by detonation spraying.
Three barrel filling levels were compared: 53%, 58%, and 63%.
Phase, microhardness, corrosion, and tribological behavior were characterized.
evidence_signal: The coatings were dominated by α-Al2O3 (77–80%), reached 1362.3 HV and 190.0 GPa at 58% filling, and reduced corrosion current density up to about twofold versus bare steel.
watch_item: Performance depends on spray parameter tuning, and wear results varied with counterbody material, so application-specific validation is needed.
paper_title: DEVELOPMENT AND CHARACTERIZATION OF WEAR-RESISTANT AL₂O₃-BASED COATINGS PRODUCED BY DETONATION SPRAYING
